businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends far beyond EV sales into FSD self-driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue reached $97.7B with 1.79M deliveries (down 1.1% YoY, the first annual decline as a public company). Q4 2025 EPS of $0.50 beat consensus ($0.45) by 10%. Key catalysts include Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, and FSD Europe approval all set for April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy generation and storage (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Over 16x the S&P 500 average (22x). Extreme premium vs auto sector (10x), but reflects AI/robotics/energy platform expectations

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on consensus EPS $2.25. 8.5x above S&P 500 forward P/E (20x), but half of TTM P/E, reflecting earnings improvement expectations

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Nearly flat growth. Deliveries of 1.79M were down 1.1%, the first annual decline. 2026 rebound is essential

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down sharply from 25%+ in 2022. Price competition and margin pressure persist. Q4 recovered to 20.1% but full-year remains low

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from FY2024's 7.24%. Price cuts and R&D expansion are the main drivers

keyMetrics.name: FCF (Free Cash Flow)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Significant improvement in cash generation despite massive investments in Terafab and Cybercab

growthDrivers.title: Bullish Chart Patterns: Harami Candle + Falling Wedge
growthDrivers.description: The 3-day chart shows a harami candlestick and falling wedge forming simultaneously, both classic reversal signals. RSI is also turning up. Bankless Times targets $415 (Fibonacci 23.6%) initially, then $500

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Chip Factory: Musk's 'Herculean Task' (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Musk called Terafab a 'Herculean task.' Joint $20-25B investment with SpaceX for a chip factory in Austin. Wedbush calls it a 'game changer' and maintains $600 target

growthDrivers.title: Europe Sales Rebound After 13 Months (Feb +11.8%)
growthDrivers.description: ACEA data shows February European registrations grew 11.8% YoY, the first increase since December 2024. However, BYD grew 100%+ in the same period

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production + Roadster Reveal in April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous robotaxi production and next-gen Roadster reveal both set for April, creating concentrated catalysts

growthDrivers.title: Netherlands FSD Approval Expected by 4/10
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expects FSD Supervised approval in the Netherlands by April 10. If approved, EU-wide expansion could follow by summer

growthDrivers.title: Bitcoin Holdings: 11,500 BTC ($815M), BTC Stable at $72K
growthDrivers.description: Tesla holds 11,500 BTC worth ~$815M. Bernstein forecasts BTC could reach $150K, adding upside to Tesla's asset value

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS Supercharger standard dominance, Terafab chip internalization

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD miles. Competitors cannot replicate this data advantage in the near term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM and others use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and loyal fanbase drive demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price advantage and rapidly closing the gap

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service across SF, Las Vegas, Austin, and Miami

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in commercial autonomous ride-hailing

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Growing global EV market share with Ioniq and EV6 on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Bankless Times targets $500: Harami candle + falling wedge pattern forming
recentNews.significance: Two bullish reversal patterns appearing simultaneously on 3-day chart. First target $415 (Fibonacci 23.6%), ultimate target $500

recentNews.headline: Musk calls Terafab a 'Herculean task,' SpaceX partnership confirmed
recentNews.significance: Yahoo Finance highlights Tesla-SpaceX joint chip factory as a monumental challenge. 2027 production target

recentNews.headline: Wedbush reiterates Terafab as 'game changer,' $600 target maintained
recentNews.significance: AI5 chip small-scale production by end of 2026, mass production in 2027

recentNews.headline: Market watches if European sales rebound can overcome competitive pressure
recentNews.significance: Simply Wall St analysis: First rebound is positive but BYD competition and margin pressure continue

recentNews.headline: Long-time bull Trip Chowdhry flips to sell, $150 target
recentNews.significance: One of Tesla's most persistent bulls switches to sell citing AI narrative collapse

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush $600 (Terafab game changer) vs Trip Chowdhry $150 (sell). 10 of 34 analysts rate sell. Meanwhile Bankless Times sees $500 on technicals

risks.title: Bulls defecting signals deteriorating sentiment
risks.description: Long-time bull Trip Chowdhry flipped to sell. 10 of 34 analysts now rate sell, a clear sign of worsening Wall Street sentiment

risks.title: Extreme valuation premium
risks.description: P/E 356x, P/S 14.6x are extreme by any benchmark. Missing expectations could trigger sharp declines

risks.title: Macro headwinds: Oil $99+, rates 3.5-3.75%
risks.description: Oil above $99 on Iran crisis, Fed rates at 3.5-3.75% with no cuts expected. Broad pressure on growth stocks

risks.title: NHTSA FSD probe escalated to 'engineering analysis'
risks.description: Federal investigation covering 3.2M vehicles upgraded one level. Recall risk increased, potentially affecting entire autonomy strategy

risks.title: First annual delivery decline + margin compression
risks.description: 2025 deliveries of 1.79M were the first decline. Automotive gross margin at 16.3% vs 25%+ in 2022

buyReasons.title: Technical reversal signals: Harami + falling wedge
buyReasons.rationale: Both patterns forming simultaneously on 3-day chart with RSI turning up. Classic reversal setup. First target $415

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration + earnings (4/28)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Netherlands (4/10), and Q1 earnings (4/28) all in April

buyReasons.title: Europe and China sales rebounding simultaneously
buyReasons.rationale: Europe up 11.8% (first in 13 months), China +35% YoY. Both key markets turning positive

buyReasons.title: FCF surge (+74%) + BTC holdings ($815M)
buyReasons.rationale: Free cash flow of $6.22B plus 11,500 BTC ($815M). Ample investment capacity

overallOpinion: Reversal signals are lighting up on the chart. The 3-day timeframe shows a harami candle and falling wedge forming simultaneously while RSI turns higher. Bankless Times targets $415 first, then $500.

April is packed with catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Netherlands approval (4/10), and Q1 earnings (4/28). Europe (+11.8%) and China (+35%) sales are rebounding together, setting up a potential convergence of technical and fundamental recovery.

Trading below both the 50-day ($389) and 200-day ($402) with RSI at 36, but patterns suggest an imminent bounce. At $386, down 23% from ATH, the setup favors a strong recovery if April catalysts deliver. Oil at $99 and rates at 3.5-3.75% remain the key headwinds.

investmentVerdict.summary: Technical reversal patterns are forming and April catalysts are concentrated. However, P/E 356x with oil at $99 and rates at 3.5-3.75% creates strong macro headwinds. Watch April events and consider accumulating below $370.
investmentVerdict.horizon: 1-2 months (April events + Q1 earnings, then reassess)

sources.description: Harami candle + falling wedge analysis, $500 target

Harami candle and falling wedge forming simultaneously on the 3-day chart. Bankless Times targets $415 then $500. RSI turning higher.

April is loaded: Cybercab production, Roadster, FSD Europe approval, Q1 earnings. Europe and China sales both rebounding. Technical and fundamental recovery may converge.

Oil at $99 and rates at 3.5-3.75% are the headwinds. Down 23% from ATH. Target $407 (+5.4%).

Target: $407 (+5.4%)
Verdict: Hold (watch April events, accumulate below $370)

witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 26) $386

Harami candle plus falling wedge. Every technical analyst's dream setup. The chart says bounce, oil at $99 says wait.

April packs Cybercab, Roadster, FSD, and earnings into one month. Wedbush sees $600, Chowdhry sees $150. Bankless Times says $500. The answer arrives in April.

empathy.hook: 23% off highs. But the chart just gave a reason for hope
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 26) $386

$499 to $386. That's a 23% drawdown and it hurts. But reversal patterns just appeared on the 3-day chart. Harami candle and falling wedge, both bullish.

April brings Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, and Q1 earnings. Sales are rebounding in Europe and China. The fundamentals haven't broken.

Oil at $99 is the obstacle. Target $407 (+5.4%). One more month of patience.
